family_summaries.lesson: Polynomially many samples can fingerprint shifts, but implemented decoding is still pair-ratio/domain-linear, full-degree, or candidate-set based.
Polynomially many samples can fingerprint shifts, but implemented decoding is still pair-ratio/domain-linear, full-degree, or candidate-set based.

rows.falsifier: A legal classical decoder recovers the shift.
rows.proof_obligation: Find a stronger algebraic decoder or prove residual ambiguity for the tested access model.

rows.falsifier: A polynomial-style decoder recovers the shift from comparable samples, pair-ratio constraints avoid domain-linear candidate scans, or the cyclotomic constraints compress to poly(log p) degree.
rows.proof_obligation: Prove a decoding lower bound showing that multiplicative-character sample fingerprints cannot be decoded in poly(log p) time, despite pair-ratio candidate filtering, full-degree cyclotomic GCD recovery, and candidate-set fingerprinting.

summary: Audited 32 multiplicative-character lower-bound rows; 22 random-sample and 28 chosen-query rows fingerprint shifts, while 23 rows are recovered by pair-ratio candidate filtering and 26 rows are recovered by full-degree cyclotomic GCD rather than a polynomial-style decoder.
